# Course Overview

`BIK 2551 Artificial Intelligence for Increased Productivity` is an executive
course at BI Norwegian Business School (7.5 ECTS, spring 2026). The course is
aimed at managers and knowledge workers who want to use generative artificial
intelligence in a structured, safe, and measurable way in their own work.

The course is being migrated to LearnForge from legacy material in
`course-inbox/ai-produktivitet/`.

## Knowledge Goals

- Understand how generative language models work, including the training
  process, tokens, probabilistic text generation, and the difference between
  search and generation.
- Know core concepts such as context windows, RAG, hallucinations, agents, and
  automation.
- Understand the ethical, legal, and security-related frameworks that shape AI
  use in organizations, including the EU AI Act, GDPR, and copyright.
- Assess maturity and risk levels for AI implementation in their own
  organization.

## Skills Goals

- Formulate effective instructions (prompts) using structured frameworks such as
  Role, Context, Task, and Format.
- Set up personal AI assistants with custom instructions and knowledge bases.
- Design and run a mini-experiment with before/after measurement of
  productivity and quality.
- Identify and map automation opportunities at different levels, from simple
  chat use to agent-based workflows.

## General Competence

- Reflect critically on the opportunities, limitations, and risks of using
  generative AI in professional practice.
- Develop and communicate an AI strategy adapted to their own team or
  organization.
- Communicate results and lessons learned through a written report and a video
  presentation.

## Planned Content

The course is organized around four in-person teaching days across two sessions:

### Session 1: Exploration and Core Skills

- **Day 1 - Introduction and language models:** What generative AI is, prompt
  engineering, ethics and law, creative AI tools, and project kick-off.
- **Day 2 - Toolkit and project start:** Context windows and RAG,
  source-grounded AI systems, custom instructions, and a project workshop with
  presentations of project ideas.

### Between Sessions (about 10 weeks)

Independent work on the project assignment. Implementation and a mini-experiment
with an AI logbook. Digital supervision via Canvas.

### Session 2: Implementation, Strategy, and the Future

- **Day 3 - AI agents:** Sharing experiences, AI agents in theory and practice,
  automation levels, and an automation workshop.
- **Day 4 - Strategy, leadership, and the future:** AI strategy and
  implementation in organizations, the future of work, and project
  presentations.

## Teaching Format

The course consists of two in-person sessions of two days each, with guided
project work in the period between them. Teaching combines academic input,
practical demonstrations, workshops, and project labs.

Throughout the course, participants work on a concrete process from their own
job and gradually develop an AI-based solution or workflow that is tested and
evaluated in practice.

## Tools

- AI platforms: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini (BI-licensed).
- Creative tools: image generation, presentation tools, multimodal models.
- Automation: no-code platforms (Zapier, Make), agent frameworks.
- No programming or Python experience required.

## Assessment

Individual project assignment with two submissions:

1. **Final report** (up to 10 pages, PDF): Problem definition, current-state
   analysis, plan for measuring effect, description of the solution,
   measurement results, benefits, limitations, and next steps.
2. **Video presentation** (5 minutes): Presentation of the project and the
   results.

Submission deadline: May 29, 2026 at 12:00.
